title: Support `syntax` parser directive in dockerfiles/containerfiles
body:
### Feature request description

Support for the `syntax` directive in dockerfiles/containerfiles that docker supports to pre-parse the file, allowing custom syntax, validation and others.

### Suggest potential solution

It would be great to implement support for the `syntax` directive to pre-parse the file and generate the final Containerfile.

### Have you considered any alternatives?

There could be other implementations, like specifying a command line flag, environment variable, etc.
But having transparent support for the same syntax used by docker would allow reusing the same files by both systems.

### Additional context

Docker supports [parser directives](https://docs.docker.com/reference/dockerfile/#parser-directives), one of the most interesting ones is `syntax`, that allows you to pre-process the file before using it, allowing you to have a custom syntax that it's converted on the fly to a proper Dockerfile/Containerfile.

One such usage is for example [Blubber](https://doc.wikimedia.org/releng/blubber/).

Example of such a file: https://gitlab.wikimedia.org/repos/cloud/cloud-vps/horizon/deploy/-/blob/main/.pipeline/blubber.yaml?ref_type=heads

Currently one way to work around this is to start a buildkit container, and build the image with buildctl passing it to the buildkit daemon before loading to podman:
```
dcaro@acme$ podman run -d --name buildkitd --privileged docker.io/moby/buildkit:latest
...
dcaro@acme$ buildctl \                                                                                                                                                                 
    --addr=podman-container://buildkitd \                                                                                                                                  
    build \                                                                                                                                                                
            --frontend=gateway.v0 \                                           
            --opt source=docker-registry.wikimedia.org/repos/releng/blubber/buildkit:v0.16.0 \
            --local context=. \                                                                                                                                            
            --local dockerfile=. \                                                                                                                                         
            --opt "filename=.pipeline/blubber.yaml"\                                                                                                   
            --opt target=production \                                                                                                       
            --output "type=docker,name=test_image" \                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      
| podman load
```

This is quite cumbersome and it does not work well when trying to use solutions that use `podman` underneath, like `podman-compose`.

comments.body: The problem is that this requires podman to fully reimplement the entire GRPC buildkit API that is used for this which is a ton of work. I believe @mheon looked into this before.
That is correct. The GRPC API is not well documented and quite extensive. Support of it would be a very large task. I'm not going to rule it out but it would require a major effort.
Would it be possible to only implement part of it for supporting only this feature? Or the whole of it would be needed anyhow? (I understood the latter, just double checking to make sure)
We don't have to do all of it, but I suspect it'd be close to 75-80% of the API spec being required, which is still substantial.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
